hypothesis: Enhancing aromatic interactions at position 5 and hydrophobic packing at position 11 will improve SSTR2 selectivity by better complementing SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 residues while avoiding off-target SSTR3/5 interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current focus on ddG binding energy is insufficient for achieving SSTR2 selectivity. The next iteration must prioritize disrupting off-target contacts at non-pharmacophore positions to increase Δmargin.

proposed_changes.rationale: Target non-pharmacophore positions where off-target contacts with SSTR3/SSTR5 are likely to occur. Disrupting these interactions will improve selectivity.
Propose mutations at focus positions to disrupt conserved pocket interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5, while preserving key pharmacophore residues (FWKT7-10) and cysteines.
